fun facts from the imperial war museum: the spike on german helmets was supposed to deflect cavalry swords, 'may god punish england' replaced 'guten tag' as a greeting, labor and capital delivering the goods, slop in the bookshop https://t.co/jOw0qL2rVU
unfortunately predictably if there was ever a play grown in a lab for me to like I can confirm it is Arcadia

“We shed as we pick up, like travellers who must carry everything in  their arms, and what we let fall will be picked up by those behind. The procession is very long and life is very short. We die on the march. But there is nothing outside the march so nothing can be lost to it. The missing plays of Sophocles will turn up piece by piece, or be written again in another language. Ancient cures for diseases will reveal themselves once more. Mathematical discoveries glimpsed and lost to view will have their time again. You do not suppose, my lady, that if all of Archimedes had been hiding in the great library of Alexandria, we would be at a loss for a corkscrew?”
